title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Automation Case: Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24
summary: A privileged self-hosted runner is reused for public pull requests, carries cloud credentials and Docker authority, drifts from its image contract, and queues releases behind unbounded jobs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci runner compatibility action node24 increment in runner isolation and capacity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify runner version compatibility for Node 24-based actions and plan staged upgrades, rollback, and GHES differences.
Model concurrency, queue time, job duration, parallelism, rate limits, storage, egress, larger runners, and cost against feedback goals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 48 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Verify runner version compatibility for Node 24-based actions and plan staged upgrades, rollback, and GHES differences. A defensible solution applies Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to runner isolation and capacity policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-runners-isolation-capacity-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-runners-isolation-capacity-5
    artifact: runner-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha runners isolation capacity 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      runner-image: ubuntu-24.04-reviewed
      action-runtime: node24
      minimum-runner: 2.327.1-or-later
      untrusted-work: github-hosted-ephemeral
      self-hosted: trusted-release-only
      network: segmented
      cleanup: single-job-destroy
      capacity: queue-budget-and-max-parallel
    verify: inspect runner version image release labels groups network authority queue duration and teardown evidence
    failure: Reject latest as immutable, reused public-PR runners, workspace cleanup as host destruction, or unbounded parallelism.
    repair: Record compatibility, isolate untrusted work, destroy ephemeral capacity, segment authority, and measure queue and cost.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
